violations.text: The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2025
Drinks were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/04/2025

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the cooler) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/04/2025

Establishment did not have a person in charge that has successfully completed an approved food safety certification course. Please email inspector proof that additional employees have been enrolled and completed an approved food safety course. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2024
Glass cleaner was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2024
Hot fudge topping was observed stored on the floor near the 2-door refrigerator. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2024

Single-service articles (plastic dome lids) were observed stored on the floor. Single-service articles must be stored at least 6 inches off the ground. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Please email inspector proof of enrollment in a food safety course. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023
The handwashing sink near the service window is leaking. A plumbing system must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2023

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment must enroll employees in a food safety course. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Written procedures were given to the person in charge during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as ice cream mix) in the refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as ice cream mix during storage. Eggs were relocated during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice was voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection (see attached disposal form). Ice machine was taken out of service and cleaned during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

The chlorine concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 10 parts per million (ppm). The concentration of chlorine in the sanitizing solution must be between 50-100 ppm. A new solution was mixed to 50 ppm during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

Yogurt was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

Chemicals were stored on the same shelf with food/equipment/single service articles in the prep area. Chemicals were relocated during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

The presence of flies is not controlled as evidenced by flies near the 3-bay sink and ice cream toppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

The door to the outside, located in the ware-washing area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023
The hot fudge and ice cream sauces are not covered. Food must be covered to prevent contamination from the premises. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023
Single-use articles (cups and other disposable items) were observed stored on the floor. Single-use articles must be stored at least 6 inches off the ground. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

Hot water was not available in the restroom handwashing sink during time of inspection. Per person in charge, hot water wasn't turned on. The person in charge turned it on during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023
Cold water was not available at the handwashing sink near the takeout window. Cold water faucet is broken. Faucet must be repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

The condensate from the air conditioning unit is draining into a the sink used for dumping and rinsing equipment. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. The person in charge redirected condensate waste into another drain during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023
